expected_output: A visually strong, image-led hotel landing page with brand-first hierarchy, full-bleed or dominant hero media, concise section narrative, clear booking action, and tasteful responsive behavior.

assertions.assertion: The first viewport makes 'Rook House' unmistakable as the primary brand or place signal.
assertions.evidence: Rook House is visible in the first viewport navigation at outputs/index.html:764 and the hero copy identifies the Porto hotel at 775-780.

assertions.assertion: The hero uses one dominant visual anchor that plausibly represents the hotel, location, rooms, hospitality, or booking experience.
assertions.evidence: The hero uses a dominant generated Porto-building visual embedded as a data SVG background at outputs/index.html:92-95.

assertions.assertion: Hero text is not placed inside a decorative card and does not rely on a generic split-screen dashboard/mockup composition.
assertions.evidence: Hero copy sits directly over the media in .hero-copy at outputs/index.html:773-780; the booking panel is separate and the layout is not a dashboard/mockup split.

assertions.assertion: The page has a coherent narrative sequence such as hero, proof/context, rooms or experience detail, and final booking CTA.
assertions.evidence: The page flows from hero/booking to house context, rooms, Porto context, and final booking CTA at outputs/index.html:824, 846, 881, and 894-895.

assertions.assertion: Copy is specific to a boutique hotel in Porto and avoids generic SaaS/startup phrasing.
assertions.evidence: Copy is specific to Porto and the hotel: Rua de Cedofeita appears at outputs/index.html:780 and the house/courtyard details at 824.

assertions.assertion: Text over media has a stable tonal area or overlay treatment that preserves contrast on desktop and mobile.
assertions.evidence: Text-over-media contrast is handled by dark overlays in the hero background at outputs/index.html:92-95 and the bottom fade at 100-106.
